data.title: VINFAST OFFICIALLY LAUNCHES TWO NEW ELECTRIC BUS MODELS IN EUROPE
data.description:
VinFast/ Key word(s): Product Launch

VINFAST OFFICIALLY LAUNCHES TWO NEW ELECTRIC BUS MODELS IN EUROPE

04.10.2025 / 14:05 CET/CEST

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

BRUSSELS, Oct. 4, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- VinFast unveiled its EB 8 and EB 12 electric bus models at Busworld Europe 2025 today. With the introduction of these two modern and intelligent public transport solutions, the Vietnamese automaker reaffirms its determination to make electric mobility accessible to everyone, expanding its presence from private passenger vehicles into the vital public transportation sector. The full-size 12-metre city bus, EB 12, already adheres to UNECE and CE regulations and is available to order in Europe. The smaller, 8-metre EB 8 will follow at a later date, and both will be protected by comprehensive unlimited mileage warranties.

Busworld provided European and international customers with their first glimpse of the EB 8 and EB 12, developed with smart technology from Vietnamese EV maker VinFast, which has already proven its capabilities in the domestic market. Both advanced models are engineered using a philosophy that optimizes performance and range, passenger comfort, pricing, and safety. They integrate a comprehensive array of advanced safety and driver assistance technologies, ensuring an ultimately secure and peaceful experience for passengers, operators and vulnerable road users on every route.

EB 8

The VinFast EB 8 is designed for optimal efficiency and agility on urban roads. It features compact dimensions (L x W x H: 8,610 x 2,470 x 3,220 mm) and a robust central electric motor delivering a maximum output of 200 kW, enabling a top speed of 80 km/h.

Utilizing a 359 kWh battery capacity, the EB 8 achieves an operational range of over 290 km per single charge, specified to exceed the typical daily requirements of buses of this size. It supports 120 kW fast charging, effectively maximizing operational uptime and schedule efficiency.

The EB 8 is configured to accommodate up to 60 passengers, including 24 seated, 1 dedicated wheelchair space, and 35 standing. Passenger access and convenience are ensured by a standard electric wheelchair ramp and a dedicated, spacious wheelchair area (1,300 mm x 750 mm).

Interior comfort is maintained by an independent, single-zone automatic climate control system. Safety features are integrated comprehensively, including Anti-lock Braking System (ABS), Electronic Brakeforce Distribution (EBD), Brake Assist (BA), and Hill-start Assist (HSA), all complemented by a full air suspension system for a high level of ride smoothness and stability.

EB 12

The VinFast EB 12 is a full-size 12-metre city bus with a footprint measuring L x W x H: 12,110 x 2,550 x 3,330 mm, a 5,900 mm wheelbase and a Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW) of 19,200 kg. Its low-floor, 3-door configuration is designed for rapid, seamless passenger movement. The EB 12 is capable of serving up to 90 passengers (35 seats, 1 wheelchair space, and 54 standing), and can be tailored to meet the requirements of each customer.

The EB 12 is equipped with powerful twin 125 kW wheelhub electric motors that deliver a combined maximum power output of 250 kW, with a top speed capped at 80 km/h.

Its 422 kWh battery has been optimized for performance, charging and cost, delivering an operational range of over 400 km per charge thanks to efficient regenerative braking technologies. The vehicle also supports 140 kW fast charging via the CCS2 port.

Premium passenger amenities include an independent, dual-zone automatic climate control system with a high-efficiency heat pump. The EB 12 also comes equipped with 26 convenient USB charging ports and an air suspension system for both the front and rear axles, delivering an exceptionally smooth driving experience.

As standard, the interior features a dedicated wheelchair space (1,300 mm x 750 mm) and an electrically operated wheelchair ramp. An LED lighting strip adds a touch of modern elegance and sophistication to the passenger cabin.

Beyond core safety systems (ABS, EBD, BA, Traction Control System - TCS, and HSA), the EB 12 is equipped with a wide range of Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) as standard. These include Driver Drowsiness and Attention Warning (DDAW) and Driver Monitoring, Blind Spot Detection, Forward and Rear Collision Warning, and Intelligent Speed Assistance (ISA). Furthermore, the bus is equipped with an Automatic Fire Suppression System and a sophisticated CCTV surveillance setup.

Ready-for-market with a leading warranty

VinFast already protects its passenger vehicle customers with industry-leading warranty cover, and it aims to mirror this commitment in the eBus market.

Both EB 8 and EB 12 will be covered by an unlimited mileage warranty of 5 years for the entire vehicle. Meanwhile, the battery and the anti-corrosion body protection are covered for up to 8 years, also with no mileage limit.

EB 12 is now available to order and will be manufactured in a state-of-the-art production facility in Vietnam that operates with a capacity of 1,500 to 2,000 vehicles per year. Commercial operation of both VinFast buses is currently scheduled for delivery and commercial operation from 2026.

With these two new product lines, VinFast will pursue strategic partnerships to establish a distribution network for its electric buses in key markets such as Germany, the Netherlands, Belgium, Sweden as well as the CEE and Baltic regions. This move is a strategic milestone that diversifies VinFast's product offerings to position the Vietnamese electric vehicle brand on the global e-mobility landscape.

Madam Le Thi Thu Thuy, Vice Chairwoman of Vingroup and Chairwoman of VinFast, stated: "VinFast was founded with the aspiration of democratizing electric mobility. Through our comprehensive product ecosystem, ranging from personal cars to buses, we stand ready to collaborate with individuals, corporate partners, and communities worldwide. We are confident that VinFast's modern, high-performance, and amenity-rich electric buses will be the ideal choice for both operators and their passengers, actively contributing to a greener and safer transportation future for all."

To demonstrate its eBus infrastructure and technologies prior to its European launch, VinFast has successfully built and operated a complete electric bus ecosystem in Vietnam. This includes manufacturing, operation, and maintenance, rapidly rolling out a decarbonized public transport network that can be accessed across major Vietnamese cities.

In the European market, VinFast has already introduced the VF 6 in the B-segment and the VF 8 in the D-segment electric SUV lineup, accompanied by highly attractive sales and after-sales policies that align closely with its customer-centric philosophy.

VinFast's strategy to expand its electric bus portfolio internationally is not only an opportunity to showcase its manufacturing strength and technological innovation, but also a clear statement of its long-term commitment to working alongside Europe's key objectives: advancing public transport, cutting emissions, and building smart, sustainable cities, while making electric mobility more accessible to all.

data.title: Will India Embrace Stablecoins? Finance Minister Sitharaman Hints at Policy Shift
data.description:
Digital currencies are changing the way the world handles money, and stablecoins are emerging as a key innovation that countries can no longer afford to ignore. These cryptocurrencies, pegged to traditional assets like the US dollar, offer faster, borderless transactions and are gaining traction among businesses and investors worldwide.

India Changing Its Shift in Crypto Policy?

India, which has always been cautious in its approach to cryptocurrencies, may now be considering a shift in its stance.

Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman recently said that nations must prepare to “engage” with stablecoins, whether they like it or not, highlighting the growing influence of digital currencies on the global financial system. 

Nirmala Sitharaman Office
@nsitharamanoffc

Watch Live: Smt @nsitharaman's inaugural address at the Kautilya Economic Conclave 2025 in New Delhi. #KEC2025 @FinMinIndia @IEGResearch https://t.co/ThjF1xrKEv

Oct 03, 2025

At the Kautilya Economic Conclave, she said that innovations like stablecoins are changing how money and capital move around the world. She warned that countries may face a clear choice: adapt to these new financial systems or risk being left behind. She also highlighted that these changes show how big the transformation in the financial world is taking place. 

“No nation can insulate itself from systematic change. Whether we welcome these shifts or not, we must prepare to engage with them,” she said. 

Her remarks indicate that India could be rethinking its approach to cryptocurrencies, keeping a close eye on how the digital economy is evolving.

India’s Approach To Crypto

Reuters recently reported that India plans to avoid fully regulating cryptocurrencies for now. The government intends to maintain partial oversight and is concerned that fully integrating digital assets into the financial system could pose systemic risks.

Back in 2021, India dropped plans to ban private digital assets. During its 2023 G20 presidency, it pushed for a global framework to regulate crypto. A 2024 discussion paper was postponed as the government waited to see how other countries approach these assets.

Although the RBI keeps warning about crypto risks, India’s market regulator, SEBI, has shown more openness to regulating digital assets.

Global Stablecoin Developments

While India is signaling changes in its stance, countries around the world are moving quickly to create rules for stablecoins. The U.S recently passed the GENIUS Act, which set up a framework for stablecoins. 

In May, Hong Kong approved a stablecoin bill that sets up a licensing system for companies issuing stablecoins backed by fiat currency. Japanese startup JPYC has also announced plans to launch the first yen-backed stablecoin later this year.

FAQs

What are stablecoins and why are they important?

Stablecoins are cryptocurrencies pegged to traditional assets like the USD, offering faster, borderless transactions for businesses and investors.

Is India changing its stance on cryptocurrencies?

India is cautiously considering engagement with stablecoins, signaling a shift toward partial oversight rather than a full ban.

How does India regulate cryptocurrencies now?

India maintains partial oversight, avoiding full integration of digital assets due to systemic risk concerns, while SEBI shows openness to regulation.

Are other countries regulating stablecoins?

Yes, the U.S., Hong Kong, and Japan are implementing frameworks and licensing systems for stablecoins to ensure safety and compliance.
